subject: [PATCH v5 25/36] mm/memory_hotplug: support N_MEMORY_PRIVATE node hotplug

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch adds support for hotplugging memory onto a private node controlled by a module. It introduces a new function, add_private_memory_driver_managed(), which allows modules to register as owners of a private node and manage its memory. The patch also updates the common __add_memory_driver_managed() function to only allow in-tree modules to hotplug N_MEMORY nodes. Private nodes are opted out of starting kswapd/kcompactd by default. If the add fails, the registration is undone. Offline_pages() clears both N_MEMORY and N_MEMORY_PRIVATE flags, while offline_and_remove_memory_ranges() drops private node registration once the last node's memory is removed.
